'''Shao to Gok''' was a member of a [[Limb/Legends|bipedal]], [[Color/Legends|yellow]]-skinned [[Species/Legends|species]] that had a pointed tail and three digits on each hand and foot. Shao to Gok's skin was covered in red blotches, and he had yellow [[Eye/Legends|eyes]]. He worked as both an information [[Scout/Legends|scout]] and an itinerant preacher. Gok was known on several outlaw [[Planet/Legends|worlds]], and teamed up with a [[Shao to Gok's companion|nameless telepath]] whose origins were considered "dubious." The [[Telepathy/Legends|telepath]] would perch itself on the preacher's shoulder. Gok made use of a [[Staff/Legends|staff]] and wore a white [[Robe/Legends|robe]].<ref name="daa" />

==Behind the scenes==
In the [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 3|third issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine]]'', published in [[1995]], the "[[Design An Alien]]" competition was launched, asking ''[[Star Wars]]'' fans to create new aliens based on the [[original trilogy]] films. 23-year-old [[Martin Schlierkamp]] entered his creation of Shao to Gok into the competition, and although it was not showcased alongside the winners of the competition in the magazine's [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 7|seventh issue]], released in [[1996]], it was published in the [[Star Wars Galaxy Magazine 10|tenth issue]] in [[1997]] as an "honorable mention."<ref name="daa" /> [[Leland Chee]], who maintains the [[Holocron continuity database]], later stated that the successfully published "Design an Alien" material was considered [[canon]].<ref name="tt">{{SWBoards|url=thread.jspa?threadID=152583&start=690#14724917|thread=Holocron continuity database questions|user=[[Leland Chee|Leland Y Chee]]|date=2006-05-03|time=5:45 PM|quote=Star Wars Galaxy Magazine #7 published the winners of the 'Design an Alien' contest, where fans were encouraged to submit artwork and backstories of new SW aliens. Are these aliens canon? [...] Yes, though any text discrepencies with other sources would likely to superceded by the other source.}}</ref>
